commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the Google Meet OAuth token success-path `response.json()` call with the shared bounded provider JSON reader and adds an oversized-response cancellation regression test.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +27. Total +28 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reaches `response.json()` for OK Google Meet OAuth token responses, while the proposed test exercises the same refresh path with an oversized stream. I did not run a live Google OAuth exchange in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor proof and normal maintainer merge review, not a narrow code repair that automation can safely supply.

target.title: fix(google-meet): bound OAuth token exchange JSON response read to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`executeGoogleTokenRequest` calls `await response.json()` as the first body read on the Google Meet OAuth token endpoint (via `fetchWithSsrFGuard`). This is unbounded ‚Äî can OOM. The error path already uses bounded `readGoogleApiErrorDetail`.

Replaces with `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap).

## Evidence

**Environment**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main

```
$ pnpm test extensions/google-meet/src/oauth.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > builds auth URLs and prefers fresh cached access tokens 40ms
 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > refreshes access tokens with a refresh-token grant 37ms
 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > refreshes cached access tokens with Date-invalid expiries 2ms
 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > falls back when refreshed token lifetimes overflow safe milliseconds 4ms
 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > bounds fallback token lifetimes when the process clock is invalid 2ms
 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > keeps explicit zero-second token lifetimes immediately stale 2ms
 ‚úì Google Meet OAuth > rejects oversized OAuth token responses and cancels the stream 47ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
```

Oversized test proves stream cancellation:
```ts
const cancel = vi.fn();
const stream = new ReadableStream<Uint8Array>({ cancel, start(controller) {
  for (let i = 0; i < 18; i++) controller.enqueue(new Uint8Array(ONE_MIB));
}});
// ... bounded reader throws, expect(cancel).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
```

## Risk

Low. 16 MiB cap matches codebase convention. Error path via `readGoogleApiErrorDetail` already bounded. Pure replacement.

ü§ñ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
